programme.title: Murderous Affairs

programme.title: Murderous Affairs

programme.title: It Takes a Killer
programme.desc: When a law school student is strangled to death in her bed, detectives discover that someone she knew extremely well had been secretly watching her for months by videotaping her through her second story window.

programme.title: It Takes a Killer
programme.desc: A recent widower is brutally murdered inside his North Dakota home; law enforcement quickly uncover evidence that reveals the homicide is part of a desperate plan from a person almost too close for suspicion.

programme.title: It Takes a Killer

programme.desc: When a house fire kills the only doctor in a small town, an arson investigation leads to the son of the doctor's nurse; the prosecutor worries this might have been a miscarriage of justice.

programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the actor, who starred in Midnight Cowboy, Deliverance and Runaway Train and won an Academy Award for his role in 1978 film Coming Home.

programme.title: UFO
programme.desc: A look at how acclaimed psychiatrist John Mack began an investigation into claims of alien abduction and the shocking discoveries he made.

programme.title: McEnroe
programme.desc: Set over the course of one night in New York, this documentary invites John McEnroe to reflect on his life and career. McEnroe's children and wife Patty Smyth offer their personal insights, enriched with intimate home video footage and previously unseen archive material from big matches.

programme.title: The Alien Autopsy Scandal
programme.desc: The documentary reveals how Hollywood producer Bob Kiviat pressed Ray and Gary about the source of the alien autopsy video as claims of a hoax grew and UFO hearings raised new questions.

programme.title: Gascoigne
programme.desc: Documentary about Paul Gascoigne, which charts his rise to fame from his humble roots in the North East to the world stage and his subsequent personal problems. The film features an extensive interview with the troubled former footballing superstar, as well as comment from those who admired his skills, including Wayne Rooney, Jose Mourinho and Gazza's ex-England and Tottenham Hotspur team-mate Gar

programme.title: The Two Escobars
programme.desc: How the fates of two Colombians - Andrés Escobar, the inspirational captain of Nacional, and Pablo Escobar, the notorious leader of the Medellin cartel - were permanently linked.

programme.title: Pantani: The Accidental Death of a Cyclist
programme.desc: Documentary about the rise and fall of Marco Pantani, the maverick Italian cyclist found dead less than six years after winning the Tour de France.

programme.title: Adam Hills: Foot Fault
programme.desc: The comedian and host of The Last Leg takes viewers behind the scenes of a global campaign to promote para-standing tennis and its potential as a future Grand Slam and Paralympic event. Adam meets and plays with former Grand Slam winners and uncovers the barriers faced by aspiring tennis players with disabilities who want to be included by their sport at the highest level.

programme.desc: A profile of the Academy Award-winning English actress, who has starred in a host of films including Julia, Mission: Impossible and Murder on the Orient Express.

programme.title: Discovering: Richard Dreyfuss
programme.desc: A glimpse into the life and career of the Oscar-winning Hollywood actor who starred in The Goodbye Girl, Jaws and Down and Out in Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and work of John Sturges, best known for directing classics The Magnificent Seven, Bad Day at Black Rock and The Great Escape.

programme.title: JFK Revisited: Through the Looking Glass
programme.desc: Oliver Stone's documentary examining the assassination of US president John F Kennedy, using James DiEugenio's 1992 book Destiny Betrayed: JFK, Cuba and the Garrison Case, and recently declassified US government files to shine a light on what happened in Dallas on November 22, 1963. Narrated by Whoopi Goldberg and Donald Sutherland.

programme.title: I Am Heath Ledger
programme.desc: Naomi Watts, Ben Mendelsohn, Ang Lee are among the contributors to a documentary celebrating the life and work of the Australian actor, who died in 2008.

programme.title: Brazil 2002
programme.desc: Documentary following the Brazilian national team through the 2002 World Cup, which culminated in the country's fifth victory in the tournament.

programme.title: My Icon: Darren Campbell
programme.desc: The Olympic gold medallist talks about his idol.

programme.title: Terry Venables: A Man Can Dream
programme.desc: A look at the life and times of the football player-turned-manager, who died in November 2023, intertwined with the story of England's unforgettable run at Euro 96, with insight from players, friends and family.

programme.title: Bobby Robson: More Than a Manager
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the career of the much-loved former England, Barcelona and Newcastle United manager. Via the Hand of God and Paul Gascoigne's tears, to titles across Europe, Robson overcame the most extreme challenges - including fighting for his life after being diagnosed with cancer - before returning to football to manage his beloved Newcastle.

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
programme.desc: The documentary charts the band's growing success with their first number one single. But as their popularity soars, so does the tabloid mania.

programme.title: Boyzone: No Matter What
programme.desc: The documentary charts the band's split which came to an end when Mikey proposed a reunion. The comeback proved a huge hit - but then tragedy struck and the tabloids descended.

programme.title: One to One: John & Yoko
programme.desc: Documentary examining the 18 months that John Lennon and Yoko Ono spent living in Greenwich Village in New York City in the early 1970s, featuring restored footage of Lennon's only full-length, post-Beatles concert.

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: A hyena goes in search of a new clan, a challenge that will require all its strength, intelligence and cunning.

programme.title: Kui Buri: Thailand's Hidden Eden
programme.desc: A look at Kui Buri National Park in Thailand, a hidden eden which is home to Asian elephants and the world's largest bovine - the gaur. Once farmland, it's now a model national park.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A balding macaque receives protection from his troop's over-enthusiastic grooming, and the park is saddened by the death of alpha male capuchin Tom, who was at the centre for seven years. Elsewhere, the chimps are given walnuts and Jestah gets more than his fair share.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Alison Cronin springs into action when an adult female chimpanzee in Mexico urgently needs the team's help. Even Lulu joins in the fun when the nursery chimps have a play session, and Kai tries to act independent and grown up while discouraging Amy from joining in his games.

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth
programme.desc: Rain welcomes baby meerkats to the Kalahari. The arrival of spring in eastern Australia ignites the passions of everything from kangaroos to humpback whales.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: Hope tries to save a fawn that has survived a snare and a bobcat attack. A baby bobcat winds up on Hope's doorstep, and an old friend returns to the rehab.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Wine ditches lessons to make a new friend. Two refugees from a Thai circus get a second chance at living semi-wild on a pre-release island.

programme.title: Big Cats Small World
programme.desc: Part two of two. Molelo's pride are exhausted when the winter floods arrive. The cheetah cubs make a difficult decision, and a scary encounter makes leopard mum Sephiri choose between her old family and her new cubs.

programme.title: Animal Empires
programme.desc: In the wild, having company means protection, while isolation often leads to danger. The documentary reveals how groups of animals work together to fight off threats.

programme.title: Animal Empires
programme.desc: Species that cannot survive in one place and must migrate, following epic flights, sea journeys and road trips that play a key part in the lives of many animals.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: The struggle for dominance in the natural world, from elands locking corkscrew horns to striped marlins spearing unsuspecting prey.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: The scavenging antics of nature's clean-up crew, as Tasmanian devils get into food fights with rivals and dung beetles roll over the competition.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: From the blond manes of golden snub-nose monkeys, to the flamingo's pink feathers, get a front-row seat to nature's most spectacular fashion show.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: For certain alphas - from gorillas and moose to pelicans and sharks - bigger is definitely better.

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: The deadly world of snake catching Down Under, where even the smallest mistake could have lethal consequences. Adele and Mick go up against an eastern brown, the second deadliest snake in the world, while Stuart and Olivia tangle with a carpet python.

programme.title: Aussie Snake Wranglers: Deadly Pursuit
programme.desc: Stuart and Olivia intercept two male brown snakes duelling for mating rights at a construction site.

programme.title: Qatar: Pearls in the Sand
programme.desc: The desert peninsula of Qatar, exploring its landscape and inhabitants, as well as its transformation from being a centre for pearls, to being known for oil and gas production.

programme.title: Africa's Wild Roommates: How Animals Share Bed and Board
programme.desc: A look at how when weaver birds, aardvarks and termites build homes in the African savannah, they find other animals moving in - creatures that all contribute something to the home.

programme.title: Wild Science
programme.desc: Tracing the brown bear's evolution from Ice Age giants to modern omnivores, and witnessing their bond with salmon and the intricate ecosystems that shape their survival.

programme.title: Monkey Island
programme.desc: The power struggles in an ancient citadel, once home to Sri Lanka's kings but now ruled over by a new dynasty - monkeys. The jungles are a monkey's paradise but not all are equal. Anna sits at the bottom of the social ladder - and with a newborn baby, life is getting tougher.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: Amber and Jon are looking for a getaway near their home in Raleigh, N.C., and Lake Gaston fits the bill.

programme.desc: A Huntsville, AL, couple seeks a weekend getaway along the shores of Guntersville Lake; the couple loves the outdoors and wants a place to spend quality time with their daughter, but they don't want to break the bank transforming their property.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: The Wabash River winds its way through Indiana and Illinois; this family is looking for the perfect lakeside fixer-upper in the southwestern part of the valley; but the pitfalls of renovation may push their project over budget.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation

programme.desc: An upstate New York couple is taking the leap in the Finger Lakes region; with their young daughter and son in tow and relatives nearby, they're excited to transform a charming fixer-upper into a place for lasting family memories.

programme.desc: The Maine Cabin Masters team faces a challenging cabin restoration after a massive tree damage to a Maine camp; they'll raise ceilings, move the kitchen for lake views, rework the staircase and remove rooms for rustic charm.

programme.desc: The Triangle House has been a staple in Phippsburg since the 20s; a local artist, who's admired the cottage since childhood, bought it in the 80s; after more than a century of wear, she invites the Maine Cabin Masters to save the local landmark.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: David and Bishop, owners of an uninhabitable cabin, ask Chase and the crew to get fun and funky with a new build with a roofline that faces the water, lots of windows to enjoy the view, and enough space to host their beloved gatherings.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: After a tree falls onto the roof of a family camp, the mishap is chosen as an opportunity; the "Maine Cabin Masters" are called in to facilitate an expansion.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Kristin's family has owned this cabin for five generations, but now would like to add on to the kitchen and raise the roof to comfortably fit her husband and son, and add more space for their daughter to enjoy the space with ease.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters

programme.title: Restoration Road With Clint Harp
programme.desc: Clint reconstructs a threshing barn into a grist mill for a Vermont bakery.

programme.desc: Maintaining a mini split and properly cleaning the indoor and outdoor components; building a garden retaining wall.

programme.title: Ask This Old House
programme.desc: The basics of dethatching and aerating; installing a smart home energy monitoring system; the different types of routers.

programme.title: This Old House

programme.title: This Old House
